results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6123 Micro-controllers & Micro-processors.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6123 Title: Micro-controllers & Micro-processors Aliases: Micro-controllers and micro-processors, Microcontrollers and Microprocessors Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a practical introduction to microcontrollers and microprocessors, assembly language programming techniques, interface hardware design, and microcontroller and microprocessor system design considerations. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Test/Quiz: 10% - Tutorial/Assignment: 30% - Final Examination: 50% Laboratory: - Familiarization with the trainer & programming fundamentals - Construction and programming of a basic electronic piano Course Contents: - Basics of microcontroller and microprocessor - The MCS-51 microcontroller - MCS-51 instruction set and assembly language programming - On-chip peripheral devices and interrupt system - Interfacing examples Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the fundamental features and operations of contemporary microcontrollers and microprocessors. - Explain the hardware architecture of a typical 8051 microcontroller. - Analyse the MCS-51 Instruction Set. - Develop assembly language programs for 8051 based embedded systems. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized punctuation/hyphenation.
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Micro-controllers & Micro-processors Aliases: Micro-controllers and micro-processors, Microcontrollers and Microprocessors Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a practical introduction to microcontrollers and microprocessors, assembly language programming techniques, interface hardware design, and microcontroller and microprocessor system design considerations. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized punctuation/hyphenation.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6144 Machine Vision & Image Processing.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6144 Title: Machine Vision & Image Processing Aliases: Machine Vision and Image Processing Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 4 Contact Hours: 61 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the basic and advanced concepts and working principles of machine vision and image processing. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Final exam: 40% Laboratory: Computer lab, Matlab/Scilab/Octive software, Arduino, Raspbery Pi, OpenCV Course Contents: - Introduction - Fundamental of image processing - Image segmentation - Image representation and recognition - Video processing - Machine vision hardware Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the fundamental concepts of machine vision, image processing and video processing. - Design suitable image segmentation, representation and recognition techniques according to a prescribed problem. - Build a prescribed machine vision system by applying software and hardware design knowledge. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Machine Vision & Image Processing Aliases: Machine Vision and Image Processing Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 4 Contact Hours: 61 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the basic and advanced concepts and working principles of machine vision and image processing. results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARA6123 Machine Learning Concepts and Technologies.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARA6123 Title: Machine Learning Concepts and Technologies Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 48 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6113 Computer and Programming Objective: To acquire the knowledge, skills and working practices in developing machine learning systems and applications. Assessment: - Written Test: 25% - Quiz: 25% - Lab Test: 10% - Assignment: 40% Laboratory: - Application of supervised machine learning for classification and regression - Unsupervised machine learning application with clustering and dimensionality reduction Course Contents: - Introduction to machine learning - Machine learning workflow - Supervised learning - Unsupervised learning Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the concepts of machine learning algorithms. - Propose solution to real-world problems using related good practices from literatures in machine learning technologies. - Develop machine learning systems and applications. - Demonstrate the application of machine learning technologies in solving real-world problems. - Demonstrate leadership, autonomy and responsibility by collaboratively solving a practical problem. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARR6163 Robot Programming.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6163 Title: Robot Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6113 Computer and Programming Objective: To acquire the knowledge, skills and working practices in developing robotic systems and applications with robot programming Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Project: 40% Course Contents: - Robot programming environment - Modeling, simulation and visualization - Sensing, vision and depth - Kinematics and control - Localization, mapping and SLAM - Navigation and path planning Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply various Robot Operating System (ROS) tools to model, simulate and visualize the development of various robotics systems. - Evaluate ROS-based integration of robotic control and perception systems using sensors and cameras. - Demonstrate the usage of basic robot navigation and path planning algorithms in mobile robots. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6163 Title: Robot Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6113 Computer and Programming Objective: To acquire the knowledge, skills and working practices in developing robotic systems and applications with robot programming Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Project: 40% Course Contents: - Robot programming environment - Modeling, simulation and visualization - Sensing, vision and depth - Kinematics and control - Localization, mapping and SLAM - Navigation and path planning Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply various Robot Operating System (ROS) tools to model, simulate and visualize the development of various robotics systems. - Evaluate ROS-based integration of robotic control and perception systems using sensors and cameras. - Demonstrate the usage of basic robot navigation and path planning algorithms in mobile robots. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6143 Linear Systems & Signals.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6143 Title: Linear Systems & Signals Aliases: Linear Systems and Signals Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARL6113 Electrical Circuits Objective: To develop skills for circuit and signal analysis as well as filter design. Assessment: - Test: 15% - Assignment: 15% - Lab Assessment: 10% - Final exam: 60% Course Contents: - Introduction to signals - Network topology - Circuit analysis using Laplace transform - State variable analysis of RLC networks - Two-port networks - Network synthesis of passive elements - Passive filters Course Learning Outcomes: - Perform signal classification, signal modelling and convolution. - Analyse electric circuits using network graphs. - Compute network parameters for two-port networks. - Solve for the time and frequency behaviour of electric circuits using Laplace Transform and state variable analysis. - Construct one-port networks and filters. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6133 Advanced Programming.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6133 Title: Advanced Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide undergraduates with a basic comprehension of the C++ programming language and the concepts of Object Oriented Programming (OOP). Assessment: - Test 1: 20% - Test 2: 20% - Assignment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Object oriented and C++ fundamentals - Object classes - Dynamic memory allocation - Inheritance and polymorphism - Basic OO design using UML - Advanced C++ concepts and applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Create objects and classes with input/output using C++ code and UML class diagrams. - Construct relationship between classes and objects using C++ code and UML class diagram. - Evaluate the usage of function templates and class templates in a program. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Advanced Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide undergraduates with a basic comprehension of the C++ programming language and the concepts of Object Oriented Programming (OOP). Assessment: - Test 1: 20% - Test 2: 20% - Assignment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Object oriented and C++ fundamentals - Object classes - Dynamic memory allocation - Inheritance and polymorphism - Basic OO design using UML - Advanced C++ concepts and applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Create objects and classes with input/output using C++ code and UML class diagrams. - Construct relationship between classes and objects using C++ code and UML class diagram. - Evaluate the usage of function templates and class templates in a program. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
